title: dithyrocarpus rothii
intro: Dithyrocarpus rothii, commonly known as the "Thai Mangosteen", is a species of evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its numerous health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its use in traditional medicine. The therapeutic actions of Dithyrocarpus rothii include treating fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions, while its bioactive constituents include xanthones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the extent of its health benefits and therapeutic applications.
parts: Dithyrocarpus rothii has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and bark. The roots of the Dithyrocarpus rothii plant are known to contain medicinal constituents like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which possess anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves contain various alkaloids, including dithyrocarpusine and rothine, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The bark of the plant contains terpenoids and glycosides, which have been traditionally used to treat fever and skin conditions.
side_effects: Dithyrocarpus rothii can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and stomach pain, due to its high alkaloid content. Prolonged exposure to its toxic compounds can also lead to neurological symptoms, including headaches and dizziness. In severe cases, improper use of the plant can cause cardiovascular problems, such as rapid heartbeat and blood pressure fluctuations. Furthermore, excessive consumption of Dithyrocarpus rothii can lead to kidney damage and other serious health issues if not monitored and used under professional guidance.
